location: Inside Lamenter's Gaol (a small dungeon in the southwest of Charo's Hidden Grave). Requires the Gaol Upper Level Key (found in a chest in the second room with Putrid Corpses) to unlock the gate. After descending the ladder, enter the room with a frozen floor on your right — a Jar Innard emerges from a side door — and loot the corpse in that room.

source.name: Corpse in the frozen-floor room, Lamenter's Gaol lower level

tactics: Get the Gaol Upper Level Key first (chest in the room with two Putrid Corpses) — you cannot reach the item without it.

After the ladder down, a Jar Innard will burst out of a side door as you enter the frost room; deal with or dodge past it, then loot the corpse for the bottle (it has no purple glow, easy to walk past).
Don't confuse this with the Gaol Lower Level Key path, which instead leads to the Lamenting Visage torch behind an illusory wall — different key, different item.
Reach the dungeon from Charo's Hidden Grave by heading northwest past the Death Rite Bird encounter, hugging the left cliff to the gaol entrance.
lore: Like the other bottles, it was reworked into a weapon by the realm's traveling perfumers, who habitually set out on journeys chasing new scents and flowers; this one was tuned to unleash a biting chill instead of a fragrance.

builds.name: Frost INT/DEX Caster
builds.why: Deals pure magic damage with even D-to-C scaling in both Int and Dex, so it slots into an intelligence-leaning caster's kit as a frostbite proc tool without needing a separate stat investment.

builds.name: Frostbite stacker
builds.why: Fixed 60 frostbite buildup per hit makes it useful paired with other cold-infused weapons or Ranni's Dark Moon-style builds to trigger the frostbite debuff (extra damage taken) faster.
